title: The Dragon's Black Gold: China seaborne coal import shows divergence in 1H24
section_path: The Dragon's Black Gold: China seaborne coal import shows divergence in 1H24
section_path_text: The Dragon's Black Gold: China seaborne coal import shows divergence in 1H24
summary:
Main
Image reference: RGB_COLOR_BRS_SHIPBROKERS_MONOGRAM_LOGO.png | assets/2024-08-13_the-dragons-black-gold-china-seaborne-coal-import-shows-divergence-in-1h24_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_ee79606c8e74.png
The latest official customs data shows that China's coal imports hit a record 249.6mln mt in 1H24, an astounding 11.9% year-on-year increase. The total import volume of thermal coal reached 192mln mt, which is 9.0% higher than the 176mln mt recorded over the same period in 2023. The total import value of China's thermal coal stood at $16bln, a 15.8% decrease year-on-year as prices fell to an average of $83.34/t, a y-o-y decrease of 22.7%.
